name: Julie Taymor
practice: Visual theater, puppet design, cross-cultural performance, spectacle as meaning
approach: Theater's power is in making the invisible visible. The Lion King's actors carry their puppets openly — the double event of actor and character creates the space for imagination. The audience does the work. The design creates the conditions for awe.
body_of_work: The Lion King (Broadway) — most commercially successful production in theater history. Titus (film), The Magic Flute (Met Opera), Spider-Man: Turn Off the Dark. Blends Indonesian shadow puppetry, Japanese Bunraku, and African ritual with Western theater.
why_instructive: She designs Keltner awe triggers deliberately — vastness (spectacle), accommodation (double event), collective effervescence (shared wonder). Her Lion King IS a sphere activation that has run nightly for 27 years.

dome_relevance.11: Creativity — cross-cultural synthesis, making invisible visible

sphere_relevance.7: Activation — the Lion King as 27-year ongoing activation, awe by design
sphere_relevance.8: Permanence — cultural permanence through theatrical innovation
sphere_relevance.10: Catalyst — her approach has influenced every major theater production since
